note: Migrate Upgrade is a Drush-only front-end to core migrate_drupal: no admin UI, permissions, or config schema. easy cases are response-graded facts about its two commands, options, and the --configure-only behavior. medium cases inspect live state that the module produces/uses (the generated migrate_plus.migration_group config entity, and the migrate_drupal_ui.performed state marker) via a setup/cleanup pair. hard cases build the migrate_plus config entities that `migrate:upgrade --configure-only` would generate and verify them live. A real Drupal-to-Drupal RUN needs an external legacy D6/D7 source database, which is not available in this environment, so the hard cases exercise the config-entity artifacts (group + upgrade_-prefixed migration) the command emits — the verifiable, DB-independent part of the workflow — rather than an actual import.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site with the migrate_upgrade module, which two Drush commands does it add, and what does each one do? Give their aliases too.
evals.expected_output: migrate:upgrade (aliases mup / migrate-upgrade) builds and runs a full Drupal-to-Drupal upgrade of a legacy D6/D7 source into the current site; migrate:upgrade-rollback (aliases mupr / migrate-upgrade-rollback) removes everything a previous upgrade imported.

evals.prompt: Running `drush migrate:upgrade`, how do I tell it where the legacy Drupal source database is? Give the two mutually-exclusive options, and the option used to locate the source's public files.
evals.expected_output: Supply the source DB with either --legacy-db-url (a Drupal 6-style DB URL like mysql://user:pw@host/db) or --legacy-db-key (a connection key already defined in settings.php); you must give exactly one. Source public files are located with --legacy-root (a site web address for HTTP, or a local filesystem path). --legacy-db-prefix handles a shared table prefix.

evals.prompt: What does the `--configure-only` flag do on `drush migrate:upgrade`, and what does `--migration-prefix` default to? Answer briefly for migrate_upgrade on Drupal 11.
evals.expected_output: --configure-only does NOT run the migrations; it exports them as migrate_plus config entities (a migration_group plus one migration per source migration) so you can edit the YAML and run a customized migration later. Generated migration ids are prefixed to avoid colliding with core plugin ids; --migration-prefix defaults to `upgrade_`.

evals.prompt: This Drupal site (drush available) has a migrate_plus migration group that was generated by a `migrate:upgrade --configure-only` run against a Drupal 7 source. Inspect the live site and report the group's machine id and its human-readable label.
evals.expected_output: A migrate_plus migration_group config entity with id `migrate_drupal_7` and label `Import from Drupal 7` (config name migrate_plus.migration_group.migrate_drupal_7), readable via `drush config:get migrate_plus.migration_group.migrate_drupal_7` or by loading MigrationGroup entities.

evals.prompt: According to migrate_upgrade, has an upgrade been performed on this site? Inspect the live site to determine it, and name the exact state key that records this (the same key `migrate:upgrade-rollback` checks).
evals.expected_output: Yes — the state key `migrate_drupal_ui.performed` is set (to a request timestamp), which is the marker a completed upgrade records and which migrate:upgrade-rollback looks for. Read it with `drush php:eval 'var_dump(\Drupal::state()->get("migrate_drupal_ui.performed"));'`.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site, create the migrate_plus migration group that `drush migrate:upgrade --configure-only` generates for a Drupal 7 source, so the config entity exists live: machine id `migrate_drupal_7`, label `Import from Drupal 7`, with its shared source connection key set to `drupal_7`. (No legacy database is attached; just create the migration_group config entity to match what the command emits.)
evals.expected_output: A migrate_plus migration_group config entity `migrate_drupal_7` (migrate_plus.migration_group.migrate_drupal_7) now exists with shared_configuration.source.key = drupal_7.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site, create the migrate_plus migration config entity that `migrate:upgrade --configure-only` would produce for core's `d7_user` migration using the default migration prefix, and assign it to the Drupal 7 upgrade group. In other words: a migration config entity whose id is the `upgrade_` prefix applied to d7_user, with its migration_group set to `migrate_drupal_7`. It only needs to load as a valid migration entity (a minimal embedded_data source / null destination is fine).
evals.expected_output: A migrate_plus migration config entity `upgrade_d7_user` (migrate_plus.migration.upgrade_d7_user) now exists, its id carries the `upgrade_` prefix, and its migration_group is `migrate_drupal_7`.
